Model-Based Meta-Analysis of Oral Acetaminophen Pharmacokinetics

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Using published aggregate data with the admixr2 R package

Author

Pramod BR

Published

July 22, 2026

Summary

This report presents a model-based meta-analysis (MBMA) of the pharmacokinetics (PK) of oral immediate-release (IR) acetaminophen (paracetamol) in healthy adults. Rather than collecting new clinical data, it pools summary-level results published in the literature and fits a single structural PK model to them using the admixr2 R package, which is built on the nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem.

Note

The current results are generated from a bridge dataset reconstructed from each study’s reported non-compartmental parameters (Cmax, Tmax, half-life). They validate the full pipeline end to end. For reportable scientific estimates, the bridge rows in data/raw/pk_profiles.csv are being replaced with curves digitized from the published figures, after which every number below regenerates automatically.

1 Objectives

By the end of this project we:

  • perform a systematic literature review;
  • extract PK summary data from published studies;
  • build an MBMA dataset;
  • fit a structural PK model with admixr2;
  • explore between-study variability;
  • evaluate dose effects and other covariates;
  • produce publication-quality figures;
  • publish the reproducible workflow on GitHub.

2 Methods

2.1 Study selection

Eligible studies reported PK of oral immediate-release acetaminophen in healthy adults, with either summary PK parameters or a digitizable mean concentration–time profile. Selection followed a PICO framework (Population = healthy adults; Intervention = oral IR tablet/capsule; Comparator = none; Outcome = PK summaries). Modified-release, effervescent and liquid formulations, disease populations, and studies with absorption-altering co-medication were excluded. Full per-paper rationale is in docs/literature_screening_log.xlsx.

2.2 Data extraction and digitization

Reported values were standardized to a single unit system (concentration mg/L, exposure mg·h/L, time h, clearance L/h). Mean concentration–time curves were digitized from published figures using WebPlotDigitizer, capturing the mean at each time, the standard deviation from error bars, and the sample size. Digitization of published figures is an accepted method in evidence synthesis and pharmacometrics.

2.3 Structural model

The base model is a one-compartment model with first-order absorption. Because only apparent parameters are identifiable from oral-only data, we estimate apparent clearance (CL/F) and apparent volume (V/F). Parameters are mu-referenced on the log scale.

Code 
oral_1cmt <- function() {
  ini({
    tka <- log(1.5); tcl <- log(21); tv <- log(60)
    prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)
    eta.ka ~ 0.09; eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v ~ 0.09
  })
  model({
    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka); cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v <- exp(tv + eta.v)
    d/dt(depot)   <- -ka * depot
    d/dt(central) <-  ka * depot - (cl / v) * central
    cp <- central / v
    cp ~ prop(prop.sd)
  })
}

2.4 Fitting with admixr2

For each study, admixr2 receives the mean concentration vector (E), its variance (V), sample size (n), observation times, and the dosing event. Fitting uses the Monte Carlo aggregate-data estimator (est = "admc"). Model selection uses the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC); covariate effects are explored by dose stratification.

3 Results

3.1 Structural parameters

Code 
params <- read_if(file.path(res, "params_oral_1cmt.csv"))
if (!is.null(params)) kable(params, digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/04_fit_model.R to generate this table.")
Fitted population PK parameters (one-compartment model), including derived exposure metrics.
parameter symbol estimate unit
Absorption rate constant (ka) ka 1.938 1/h
Apparent clearance (CL/F) CL/F 12.662 L/h
Apparent volume of distribution (V/F) V/F 42.040 L
Elimination rate constant (ke) ke 0.301 1/h
Elimination half-life (t1/2) t1/2 2.301 h
Time to peak at 1000 mg (Tmax) Tmax 1.137 h
Peak concentration at 1000 mg (Cmax) Cmax 16.888 mg/L
Exposure at 1000 mg (AUC0-inf) AUC0-inf 78.974 mg*h/L

3.2 Structural model comparison

Code 
cmp <- read_if(file.path(res, "structural_model_comparison.csv"))
if (!is.null(cmp)) kable(cmp, digits = 2) else cat("Run scripts/04b_two_compartment.R to generate this table.")
One- vs two-compartment comparison. Lower AIC indicates the preferred model.
model n_par obj_2LL AIC dAIC
1-compartment 3 2383.56 2397.56 0.00
2-compartment 5 2381.93 2399.93 2.37

The one-compartment model is preferred: the two-compartment version does not lower the AIC enough to justify its additional parameters.

3.3 Dose-stratified covariate analysis

Code 
cov <- read_if(file.path(res, "covariate_stratified.csv"))
if (!is.null(cov)) kable(cov, digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/05_covariate_analysis.R to generate this table.")
Model parameters estimated within dose strata.
subgroup n_arms ka_per_h clf_L_h vf_L obj_2LL AIC
All studies 5 1.938 12.66 42.04 2383.56 2397.56
Low dose (<=700 mg) 4 2.747 13.36 47.61 1277.99 1291.99
High dose (>=1000 mg) 2 0.321 18.10 14.26 628.86 642.86

A shift in apparent parameters across dose strata is consistent with the known dose-dependence of oral acetaminophen bioavailability (Rawlins, 1977).

3.4 Diagnostic figures

if (!is.null(conc)) kable(head(conc, 12), digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/06_plots.R to generate this table.")

S01 500 IR tablet 0.50 2.154 0.539 6 6.770 -4.616 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 0.75 2.687 0.672 6 7.943 -5.256 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 1.00 2.991 0.748 6 8.392 -5.401 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 1.50 3.159 0.790 6 8.194 -5.035 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 2.00 3.008 0.752 6 7.418 -4.410 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 3.00 2.391 0.598 6 5.663 -3.272 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 4.00 1.767 0.442 6 4.215 -2.448 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)
S01 500 IR tablet 6.00 0.902 0.226 6 2.311 -1.409 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)

S01 500 IR tablet 12.00 0.111 0.028 6 0.379 -0.268 Rawlins 1977 (F,CL real; V,ka typical)

4 Limitations

  • Current estimates use a bridge dataset; final results require digitized real curves.
  • The literature search was a rapid scoping pass; a formal PubMed + Embase search with two-reviewer screening is planned before publication.
  • Because publications rarely report cross-time covariance, study variance is treated as a diagonal (per-time) matrix — a standard, mildly conservative approximation.

5 Reproducibility

The complete workflow runs with source("scripts/run_all.R"). Package versions are recorded in results/sessionInfo.txt. Source code and documentation are available at https://github.com/pbr26/MBMA_Acetaminophen_PK.

```{r setup}
#| include: false
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(here)

# Helper: read a results file if it exists, else return NULL (keeps the report
# rendering even before every script has been run).
read_if <- function(path) if (file.exists(path)) readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE) else NULL

res <- here::here("results")
plt <- here::here("plots")
```

# Summary {.unnumbered}

This report presents a **model-based meta-analysis (MBMA)** of the pharmacokinetics
(PK) of oral immediate-release (IR) acetaminophen (paracetamol) in healthy adults.
Rather than collecting new clinical data, it pools **summary-level results published
in the literature** and fits a single structural PK model to them using the
[`admixr2`](https://cran.r-project.org/package=admixr2) R package, which is built on
the `nlmixr2`/`rxode2` ecosystem.

::: {.callout-note}
The current results are generated from a **bridge dataset** reconstructed from each
study's reported non-compartmental parameters (Cmax, Tmax, half-life). They validate
the full pipeline end to end. For reportable scientific estimates, the bridge rows in
`data/raw/pk_profiles.csv` are being replaced with curves digitized from the published
figures, after which every number below regenerates automatically.
:::

# Objectives

By the end of this project we:

- perform a systematic literature review;
- extract PK summary data from published studies;
- build an MBMA dataset;
- fit a structural PK model with `admixr2`;
- explore between-study variability;
- evaluate dose effects and other covariates;
- produce publication-quality figures;
- publish the reproducible workflow on GitHub.

# Methods

## Study selection

Eligible studies reported PK of **oral immediate-release acetaminophen in healthy
adults**, with either summary PK parameters or a digitizable mean concentration–time
profile. Selection followed a PICO framework (Population = healthy adults;
Intervention = oral IR tablet/capsule; Comparator = none; Outcome = PK summaries).
Modified-release, effervescent and liquid formulations, disease populations, and
studies with absorption-altering co-medication were excluded. Full per-paper rationale
is in `docs/literature_screening_log.xlsx`.

## Data extraction and digitization

Reported values were standardized to a single unit system (concentration mg/L, exposure
mg·h/L, time h, clearance L/h). Mean concentration–time curves were digitized from
published figures using **WebPlotDigitizer**, capturing the mean at each time, the
standard deviation from error bars, and the sample size. Digitization of published
figures is an accepted method in evidence synthesis and pharmacometrics.

## Structural model

The base model is a **one-compartment model with first-order absorption**. Because only
apparent parameters are identifiable from oral-only data, we estimate apparent clearance
(CL/F) and apparent volume (V/F). Parameters are mu-referenced on the log scale.

```{r model-spec}
#| eval: false
oral_1cmt <- function() {
  ini({
    tka <- log(1.5); tcl <- log(21); tv <- log(60)
    prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)
    eta.ka ~ 0.09; eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v ~ 0.09
  })
  model({
    ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka); cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v <- exp(tv + eta.v)
    d/dt(depot)   <- -ka * depot
    d/dt(central) <-  ka * depot - (cl / v) * central
    cp <- central / v
    cp ~ prop(prop.sd)
  })
}
```

## Fitting with admixr2

For each study, `admixr2` receives the mean concentration vector (E), its variance (V),
sample size (n), observation times, and the dosing event. Fitting uses the Monte Carlo
aggregate-data estimator (`est = "admc"`). Model selection uses the Akaike Information
Criterion (AIC); covariate effects are explored by dose stratification.

# Results

## Structural parameters

```{r params}
#| tbl-cap: "Fitted population PK parameters (one-compartment model), including derived exposure metrics."
params <- read_if(file.path(res, "params_oral_1cmt.csv"))
if (!is.null(params)) kable(params, digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/04_fit_model.R to generate this table.")
```

## Structural model comparison

```{r struct-cmp}
#| tbl-cap: "One- vs two-compartment comparison. Lower AIC indicates the preferred model."
cmp <- read_if(file.path(res, "structural_model_comparison.csv"))
if (!is.null(cmp)) kable(cmp, digits = 2) else cat("Run scripts/04b_two_compartment.R to generate this table.")
```

The one-compartment model is preferred: the two-compartment version does not lower the
AIC enough to justify its additional parameters.

## Dose-stratified covariate analysis

```{r cov}
#| tbl-cap: "Model parameters estimated within dose strata."
cov <- read_if(file.path(res, "covariate_stratified.csv"))
if (!is.null(cov)) kable(cov, digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/05_covariate_analysis.R to generate this table.")
```

A shift in apparent parameters across dose strata is consistent with the known
dose-dependence of oral acetaminophen bioavailability (Rawlins, 1977).

## Diagnostic figures

::: {layout-ncol=1}
![Observed means (points) with model fit (line), by study and dose.](plots/fit_profiles.png){#fig-profiles}

![Observed vs population-predicted concentrations.](plots/obs_vs_pred.png){#fig-ovp}

![Residuals versus time.](plots/residuals_time.png){#fig-resid}
:::

## Observed vs predicted concentrations

```{r conc-head}
#| tbl-cap: "First rows of the observed vs predicted concentration table (full table in results/)."
conc <- read_if(file.path(res, "concentration_obs_pred.csv"))
if (!is.null(conc)) kable(head(conc, 12), digits = 3) else cat("Run scripts/06_plots.R to generate this table.")
```

# Limitations

- Current estimates use a **bridge dataset**; final results require digitized real curves.
- The literature search was a **rapid scoping pass**; a formal PubMed + Embase search with
  two-reviewer screening is planned before publication.
- Because publications rarely report cross-time covariance, study variance is treated as a
  **diagonal** (per-time) matrix — a standard, mildly conservative approximation.

# Reproducibility

The complete workflow runs with `source("scripts/run_all.R")`. Package versions are
recorded in `results/sessionInfo.txt`. Source code and documentation are available at
<https://github.com/pbr26/MBMA_Acetaminophen_PK>.

```{r session}
#| code-fold: true
sessionInfo()
```
